# About Browser Token Validators

Zscaler SDK for Mobile Apps Help 
Browser Access 
About Browser Token Validators
Zscaler SDK for Mobile Apps
About Browser Token Validators
Ask Zscaler

Similar to token validators, browser token validators inspect and validate JSON Web Tokens (JWTs) issued by your identity provider. During validation, ZSDK verifies token validity, signing certificate authenticity, and required token claims (e.g., subject, issuer, and audience). The Browser Token Validator page lists browser token validators for Browser Access-enabled application segments.

Browser token validators provide the following benefits and enable you to:

Enforce Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) by validating user identity prior to granting access.
Establish secure communication channels only after successful token validation.
Specify which browser token validator is used for a Browser Access-enabled application segment.

About the Browser Token Validator Page

On the Browser Token Validator page (Authentication > User Authentication > Browser Token Validator), you can do the following:

Filter by Name to search for a specific browser token validator.
Refresh the page.
Add a browser token validator.
View a list of browser token validators. For each browser token validator, you can see:
Name: The name of the browser token validator.
Type: The type of the browser token validator.
Edit a browser token validator.
Delete a browser token validator.
Customize the columns to display.
